briefing.opening: Good morning. We will now begin the climate briefing for May 30, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Global Climate Crisis Warning and Intensification of Heatwaves
sections.content: In Europe, a very large and powerful heat dome has emerged, rewriting heatwave history [1], and overwhelming new temperature records are rapidly appearing across various parts of the world [4]. The World Meteorological Organization (WMO) has warned that there is a possibility of record-high temperatures being recorded by 2027, indicating that the Earth has entered a new danger zone [7]. Such climate change is extending Europe's hot season, causing schools to suffer from high temperatures [26], and affecting international events such as the French Open tennis tournament [27]. Furthermore, a Nature study has revealed that the global occurrence of larger hailstones is linked to climate change, adding to the severity of extreme weather phenomena [33].

sections.title: ⚠️ Threats to Ecosystems and Changes in International Order
sections.content: Beyond mere temperature increases, the climate crisis is causing shifts in the international order, with analyses suggesting the need to prepare for 'new imperialism' [2]. Moreover, climate change is threatening plant species worldwide through habitat reduction [29], and a serious warning from a University of Chile study indicates that black kelp forests will decrease by 58% by 2050 [34]. This is an issue that could shake the foundation of ecosystems and have significant impacts on food security.

sections.title: 🌍 Global Climate Regulation Retreat
sections.content: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has announced a draft proposal to revoke climate change regulations from the Biden era, signaling a retreat in international climate policy [31]. The SEC is moving towards abolishing rules that mandate corporate reporting of greenhouse gas emissions and climate risks [32], and a top Wall Street official from the Trump camp reportedly thwarted Biden-era climate regulations targeting U.S. companies [35]. These developments could increase uncertainty regarding corporate climate responsibility and negatively impact global carbon reduction efforts.

sections.title: 🌱 Active Domestic Climate Adaptation and Carbon Neutrality Efforts
sections.content: Domestically, diverse efforts are underway to adapt to climate change and achieve carbon neutrality. Jindo County is establishing a 'smart seaweed seed production base' [3], and Jinan County is proactively responding to sudden pest outbreaks and supporting joint control agents [5], showcasing notable adaptation efforts in the agricultural sector. The Korea Meteorological Administration (KMA) is significantly innovating its climate crisis response system, promoting improvements that encompass everything from weather advisories to renewable energy services [6], and the citizen safety net is being strengthened, with climate insurance providing 150,000 won in support for heatwave-related illnesses being offered to 14.4 million people [8]. Furthermore, the government plans to finalize and implement detailed carbon neutrality roadmaps for each sector within this year [15], and local-level carbon neutrality initiatives are also active, with candidate Seong Gwang-jin presenting a vision to transform Daejeon into a carbon-neutral educational city [9, 13, 16]. The Mungyeong Neulbom Family Volunteer Group is practicing carbon neutrality by throwing EM soil balls [11], and efforts to spread carbon neutrality through citizen participation and education, such as Soonchunhyang University and the Eco Education Volunteer Network hosting a 'Carbon Neutrality Drawing Contest & Eco Plogging' event, are also noteworthy [12, 14].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerated Renewable Energy Transition and Regional Coexistence Models
sections.content: The transition to renewable energy is accelerating both domestically and internationally, producing positive ripple effects when combined with regional coexistence models. Sejong Facilities Corporation and Korea Midland Power are collaborating on solar power generation projects, simultaneously pursuing carbon neutrality and regional coexistence [17, 23], and Spain and France are creating new European standards that simultaneously promote renewable energy expansion and ecosystem protection [18]. In the Philippines, diesel power plants are being converted to solar power [19], and in Africa, power investments are shifting from coal and large-scale hydropower to solar power and BESS (Battery Energy Storage Systems) [22]. Domestically, Mayor Oh Se-hoon announced a goal to completely phase out old diesel vehicles by 2030 and increase the renewable energy achievement rate to 21% [20], and Jeonbuk Governor candidate Lee Won-taek pledged to return renewable energy profits to the region for use as citizens' pensions and income [21, 25]. Candidate Cheon Ho-seong also announced the 'Jeonbuk Education RE100' pledge, which includes reducing school electricity bills and expanding solar power, demonstrating that renewable energy is establishing itself as a driving force for regional economic revitalization beyond environmental protection [24].

briefing.closing: The climate-related news gathered today simultaneously highlights global crisis warnings, ongoing adaptation and carbon neutrality efforts both domestically and internationally, and the importance of renewable energy transition. This concludes today's climate briefing.
